title: Nulan VI

{{Quote|Unpopular among the older pilots, ''[Captain Tomax Bren's]'' changes first proved their effectiveness at the siege of Nulan VI, where Scimitar's deep-atmospheric precision bombing forced the capitulation of the rebellious capital.|A compilation of Galactic Empire documents by Major Arhul Hextrophon|<ref name="IS2" />}}
'''Nulan VI''' was a celestial body<ref name="IS2" /> situated in the [[Nulan system/Legends|Nulan system]] of the [[Hook Nebula/Legends|Hook Nebula]],<ref name="EAOC" /> a part of the [[Trailing Sectors/Legends|Trailing Sectors]] portion of<ref name="TEA" /> the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]].<ref name="EAOC" /> It was the site of a [[Unidentified Nulan VI capital|capital]] that, at some point by the time shortly after the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], rebelled against the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] and allied itself with the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]].<ref name="IS2" />

In response, as part of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War's]] [[Hook Nebula campaign]], the Imperial [[Qeimet fleet]] [[Siege of Nulan VI|besieged]] Nulan VI, and the [[Fleet/Legends|fleet's]] [[Scimitar assault wing]] carried out deep-[[Atmosphere/Legends|atmospheric]] precision [[Bomb/Legends|bombing]] that forced the [[Capital/Legends|capital]] to surrender. The siege of Nulan VI was subsequently mentioned in a compilation of Imperial documents made by Rebel [[Historian/Legends|historian]] [[Major/Legends|Major]] [[Arhul Hextrophon/Legends|Arhul Hextrophon]] for Alliance [[Supreme Commander (Alliance)/Legends|Supreme Commander]] [[Mon Mothma/Legends|Mon Mothma]] and other Rebel [[Officer/Legends|officers]].<ref name="IS2" />

==Behind the scenes==
Nulan VI was first mentioned in [[Greg Gorden]]'s [[1989]] ''[[Imperial Sourcebook]]'' that was published by [[West End Games]] for use with ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''.<ref name="ISB">''[[Imperial Sourcebook]]''</ref> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Nulan [[Star system/Legends|system]], and therefore Nulan VI, in grid square O-18.<ref name="TEA" /> "[[Twinkle, Twinkle Little Star, How I Wonder Where We Are]]," a [[1990]] [[roleplaying game]] source article that was published in the [[Voyages SF 13|thirteenth issue]] of the ''[[Voyages SF]]'' magazine<ref name="Twinkle">{{VoyagesCite|13|Twinkle, Twinkle Little Star, How I Wonder Where We Are}}</ref> and was [[Canon|non-canonical]] with respect to the [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] continuity,<ref name="Unlicensed">{{UnlicensedStatus}}</ref> placed the Nulan system in the [[Ammuud sector]].<ref name="Twinkle" />
